title: FAIRplex Data Harmonization Procedures
body:
Introduction

The National Institutes of Health (NIH) Accelerating Medicines Partnership® (AMP®) program produces high-value biomedical data across many disease areas.

SysBio FAIRplex, a FAIR Platform for Exploration of Systems Biology funded by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) Common Fund Venture Program, aims to speed scientific discovery by making it easier to explore and analyze these data across diseases. It aims to develop a scalable, interoperable approach to harmonize clinical, biospecimen, and omics data from multiple AMP® projects.

SysBio FAIRplex brings together data from several AMP® projects spanning a wide range of disease domains, including Alzheimer’s disease (AMP® AD), autoimmune disorders (AMP® AIM), amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (AMP® ALS), heart failure (AMP® HF), and others. It also supports multiple data types, as shown in the table below.

AMP® Project Status: Active/Completed Disease Area Primary Focus Major Data Types and Sources - currently or soon to be available through the AMP
AMP® AD (1&2) AMP® AD 1 - Completed

AMP® AD 2 - Active		 Alzheimer’s Disease Target and biomarker discovery using deep multi-omic and clinical data Raw and processed data from the disease target and peripheral tissue: Clinical, genetics, transcriptomics (bulk and single cell), epigenomics, proteomics, metabolomics/lipidomics
AMP® AIM Active Autoimmune & Immune-Mediated Diseases Deep cellular and spatial mapping of autoimmune disease mechanisms Will include raw and processed data from disease targets: Clinical, genetics, and spatial transcriptomics
AMP® ALS Active Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis Biomarker and target discovery; progression modeling Individual level raw and processed data from experimental and natural history studies and clinical trials, including epigenomics, transcriptomics, clinical data, including longitudinal datasets indexed from various repositories
AMP® BGTC Active Bespoke Gene Therapy Consortium - Rare genetic diseases Platforms and standards for tailored gene therapies Data arising from gene therapy development, evaluation, and trials
AMP® CMD Active Common Metabolic Diseases Genetic and functional drivers of metabolic diseases Aggregate summary data from a variety of tissues: genetics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics.

Individual level processed transcriptomics and epigenomics
AMP® HF Active Heart Failure Phenotyping and precision medicine framework Raw and processed data from blood and a variety of tissues: Clinical, genetics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, proteomics, metabolomics, imaging
AMP® PDRD Active Parkinson’s Disease and Related Disorders Biomarker discovery and subgroup characterization Raw and processed data from disease target and peripheral tissue: Clinical, genetics, transcriptomics, proteomics
AMP® RA/SLE Completed Rheumatoid Arthritis and Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Molecular “disease deconstruction” using single-cell & multi-omic analyses of tissue and blood (foundation for AMP® AIM) Raw and processed data from disease target tissue: Clinical, genetics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, proteomics, cytometry
AMP® SCZ Active Schizophrenia Mechanism and early-risk characterization Clinical, behavioral assessments, neurophysiology, neuroimaging, wearable devices, interview transcripts
AMP® T2D Completed Type 2 Diabetes Development of resources to improve the development of new therapies for complex diseases, such as diabetes and diabetic complications (foundation for AMP® CMD) Aggregate summary data from a variety of tissues: genetics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics.

Individual level processed transcriptomics and epigenomics

Harmonization Methods

To provide a single entry point for researchers to query and compare data, SysBio FAIRplex is harmonizing clinical data, sample and assay metadata, and a subset of data generated from omics assays into a shared format. This harmonization generates three primary outputs:

  1. Common Data Elements (CDEs): Tabular clinical data, sample, and assay metadata aligned to a universal schema mapped to a data model framework.
  2. Harmonized Individual Level Files: Specific files (e.g., genomic variant calls, gene counts per sample) stored in Hierarchical Data Format version 5 (HDF5) leveraging self describing schema and detailed documentation for FAIR (i.e., findability, accessibility, interoperability, and reusable) and transparency. Genetic data will be made available in standard plink2 binary dosages.
  3. Genomic Summary Results: Aggregated results (e.g., genome-wide association studies, principal component analysis) made available for exploration through SysBio FAIRplex visualization tools also in HDF5 format.

All harmonizations are performed using documented standard pipelines that will be made publicly available through the SysBio FAIRplex code repository. The resulting HDF5 (Hierarchical Data Format version 5) files are returned to the individual AMP® projects from which the data originated, and indexed in the SysBio FAIRplex platform. The harmonized data are accompanied by pipeline quality assessments and versioned READMEs describing the source input data and methods.

Visualizations and Tools

The Visualization and Tools pages provide interactive ways to explore harmonized data generated through SysBio FAIRplex across multiple AMP® projects. It includes tools to summarize cohort demographics and phenotypes, visualize differential gene expression from harmonized single-cell/single-nucleus RNA-seq data, and view genome-wide association studies (GWAS) results from harmonized genetics analyses. These pages will expand with additional datasets over time.

Data Summary Browser

The Data Summary Browser summarizes cohort demographics and phenotypes for AMP® AD, AMP® CMD, AMP® PDRD, and AMP® RA/SLE where SysBio FAIRplex has generated the first phase of harmonized single-cell RNA-sequencing. Summaries are provided in alignment with AMP® projects’ data governance policies, using clinical and demographic variables available from the source AMP®.

Key features

  • Select one or more AMP® project(s) to begin
  • View summaries with large pie charts showing each variable across the full selected cohort (all selected AMP® programs)
  • View smaller pie charts showing each variable within each individual selected AMP® project

Gene Expression Browser

Through the Gene Expression Browser, SysBio FAIRplex enables cross-disease transcriptomic insights by harmonizing bulk and single cell/single nuclei RNA-seq data from AMP® AD, AMP® CMD, AMP® PDRD, and AMP® RA/SLE into a shared analytical framework that pools cohorts using a common reference and consistent Quality Control (QC) filters. Documented transcriptomics harmonization and analysis pipelines will be made publicly available through a SysBio FAIRplex code repository as the program expands in the future.

Key features

  • Select a comparison type, AMP® project(s), and cell type to view results
  • View differentially expressed genes in an interactive volcano plot
  • Hover to see more information for each gene
  • Click a gene to view details in the table below
  • View top enriched annotations for upregulated or downregulated genes in the module at the bottom of the page

These pipelines use gene count matrices as inputs. Each sample was pseudobulked by taking the sum (or average) per-cell counts within each annotated cell type to create one pseudobulk profile per sample × cell type. This makes the data better suited for case/control comparisons by producing one value per gene per cell type per sample, rather than many noisy single-cell measurements. The pipeline used two tools from the Raychaudhuri lab at the Broad Institute and Brigham and Women's Hospital. Code and technical documentation can be found on GitHub.

  • Harmony1 is an iterative integration method. It starts from a Principal Component Analysis (PCA) embedding of cells across datasets and learns cell-specific linear corrections that bring matching cell populations together while preserving real biological differences. It is designed to scale to hundreds of thousands of cells and to handle multiple overlapping covariates (for example, donor, platform, and cohort).
  • Symphony2 supports single-cell reference mapping. After Harmony creates an integrated reference, Symphony compresses it into a lightweight object so new datasets can be mapped in seconds without re-running integration. This enables new AMP® datasets to be added later without re-harmonizing everything.

Together, these tools create a reusable reference: build it once with Harmony, then map new data into it with Symphony. The rest of the pipeline (QC, pseudobulking, differential expression) is built around this core concept.

Genetics Studies

SysBio FAIRplex enables cross-disease genetic insights by harmonizing whole-genome sequencing (WGS) data from multiple AMP® projects into a shared analytical framework that pools cohorts using a common reference, consistent Quality Control (QC) filters, shared ancestry adjustment, and a consistent case-control definition. A documented genetics harmonization and analysis pipeline will be made publicly available through a SysBio FAIRplex code repository as the program expands in the future.

The Genetics Studies tool lets you explore visualizations of genome-wide association studies (GWAS) within cohorts from AMP® AD and AMP® PDRD. Case-versus-case GWAS were performed on 561 individuals with Alzheimer’s disease (AD) from AMP® AD versus 2,542 individuals with Parkinson’s disease or related disorders from AMP® PDRD.

Key features

  • Manhattan plot: x-axis shows the genome with chromosomes laid end-to-end (ordered by chromosome number); y-axis shows −log10(p-value); each point is a variant
  • Q–Q plot: x-axis shows expected quantiles under a uniform distribution; y-axis shows the observed values

The GWAS analysis utilizes called variants, which were derived from the variant call format (VCF) or PLINK-format genotype files alongside per-sample covariates such as age, sex, principal components of ancestry, and disease status. For the available public data, the genetic analysis was limited to European-ancestry samples. This was a deliberate scoping decision to keep comparisons across AMP® data sources well powered and to reduce confounding from ancestry-specific allele frequency differences while validating the pipeline end-to-end. Expanding to additional disease focuses and ancestry groups is planned as a next step.

Sources

  1. Patikas N, Yao H, Korsunsky I, Hemberg M, Millard N, Fan J, Slowikowski K, Raychaudhuri S (2026). harmony: Fast, Sensitive, and Accurate Integration of Single Cell Data. R package version 2.0.2, https://github.com/immunogenomics/harmony.
  2. Kang, J.B., Nathan, A., Weinand, K. et al. Efficient and precise single-cell reference atlas mapping with Symphony. Nat Commun 12, 5890 (2021). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-25957-x

Additional Resources

For additional related resources, see below.

  • AMP® Project Data Portals: Individual AMP® project datasets and harmonization initiatives, such as AMP® AD and AMP® AIM, are available via AMP-specific portals. See how to access AMP® Project Data for access details.
